data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/21/25 – Futures Dip After Thursday's Market Losses
data.description:
U.S. stock futures declined slightly on Friday morning after all major indices closed lower yesterday. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.48%, 0.44%, and 0.42%, respectively, at 4:40 a.m. EST, March 21.

In Thursday’s trading session, the S&P 500, the Nasdaq Composite (NDAQ), and the Dow Jones declined 0.2%, 0.3%, and 0.03%, respectively.

Investor concerns stem from the Federal Reserve’s recent policy announcements. While the Fed reaffirmed its forecast for two rate cuts this year, it also raised its inflation outlook and expects slower economic growth. This mix is causing fears of “stagflation,” which means slow growth and rising prices.

Adding to market uncertainty are President Trump’s proposed tariff policies, which Fed Chair Jerome Powell noted could impede progress on inflation.

Meanwhile, the Nasdaq remains in correction territory, down more than 10% from its recent peak.

In after-hours trading, Nike (NKE) and FedEx (FDX) stocks fell after reporting disappointing quarterly results. However, Micron Technology (MU) was in the green after it reported strong Q2 results.

Today, investors await results from some major companies such as Nio (NIO) and Carnival (CCL).

At the time of writing, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was up, floating near 4.239%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending higher, hovering near $68.32 per barrel as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indices opened lower today as investors assessed recent monetary policy decisions from central banks in Europe and the U.S.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower on Friday

Asia-Pacific indices were in the red today as traders reacted to U.S. economic data and were worried over Trump's tariffs. The upcoming April 2 deadline for reciprocal tariffs has added to global trade fears.

At the same time, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index was down 2.19%. Further, China's Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component indices declined 1.29% and 1.76%, respectively. Also, Japan's Nikkei index was down 0.2%, while the Topix index was up 0.29%.

Interested in more economic insights? Tune in to our LIVE webinar.

Disclosure

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Analysts Are Bullish on Precigen Stock (PGEN) Amid Positive Pipeline Developments
data.description:
Precigen (PGEN) reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings and is making strides toward commercializing its lead asset, PRGN-2012, for recurrent respiratory papillomatosis. The FDA has granted priority review to the company’s Biologics License Application (BLA) for PRGN-2012, with a decision expected by August 2025. Financially, while the company has faced increased research and development costs linked to PRGN-2012, it ended 2024 with a cash position that extends its runway into 2026. Analysts maintain a positive outlook on the stock, citing its promising pipeline and readiness for a prospective product launch.

Promising Pipeline Developments

Precigen is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing advanced gene and cell therapies. The company applies precision technology to tackle challenging diseases, particularly in immuno-oncology, autoimmune disorders, and infectious diseases. Precigen is advancing a diverse portfolio of unique therapies through preclinical and clinical stages, aiming to achieve clinical proof-of-concept and eventual commercialization.

The recent developments involving PRGN-2012, aimed at treating recurrent respiratory papillomatosis, mark a substantial step forward in the treatment's clinical progression. Precigen’s BLA has been accepted by the FDA without needing an advisory committee meeting, indicating a smooth path toward approval. This treatment has the potential to become the first therapy of its kind to gain FDA approval, thanks to its strong efficacy, safety profile, and ease of administration.

In preparation for a potential 2025 commercial launch, Precigen is actively advancing its commercial and manufacturing readiness. Financially, the Company is in a stable position with $97.9 million in cash and investments, which is projected to support its operations well into 2026, even beyond the anticipated introduction of PRGN-2012 to the market.

For the fiscal year 2024, the company faced a challenging financial landscape, with total revenue decreasing by 37%, or $2.3 million, primarily due to a reduction in product and service volumes at Exemplar. While the company marked a decrease in costs for products and services, it increased spending in research and development, which rose by $4.5 million due to clinical trials, manufacturing material costs, and commercial readiness planning. This contributed to the net loss widening to $126.2 million.

Analysts are Bullish

Analysts following the company remain bullish on its prospects. For instance, H.C. Wainwright analyst Swayampakula Ramakanth and JMP Securities analyst Jason Butler both maintain a Buy rating on shares of PGEN with a price target of $6.00 due to recent promising developments.  Clinical trial successes, particularly with PRGN-2012, and ongoing developments in its pipeline, such as PRGN-2009 and PRGN-3006, reinforce confidence in its growth potential. These factors, along with strategic facility upgrades and partnerships, support an optimistic outlook for revenue growth.

Precigen is rated a Strong Buy overall, based on the recent recommendations of three analysts. The average price target for PGEN stock is $6.33, which represents a potential upside of 251.67% from current levels.

Disclosure

data.author: Casey Dylan, CIMA

data.title: Accenture (ACN) Warns of Revenue Hit as Musk's DOGE Cuts Bite, Analysts Slash Targets
data.description:
Global consulting firm Accenture (ACN) has warned of significant revenue risks due to budget cuts under the Elon Musk-led Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). In its post-earnings call, Accenture stated that the DOGE's cost-cutting measures have led to delays and cancellations of its new contracts, impacting its sales. ACN stock dropped over 7% on Thursday as concerns about reduced government spending and a weak 2025 guidance overshadowed the positive Q2 results.

In context, DOGE is a cost-cutting initiative under Trump's second term that aims to cut federal spending and downsize the workforce.

Accenture Faces Headwinds

Accenture is among the first to highlight the revenue impact of DOGE's tightened spending, citing slowed procurement processes.

Moreover, the company stated that the new contracts with the U.S. government have significantly declined since President Trump took office in January. A recent federal filing revealed that an Accenture contract, which could have generated up to $5 million in additional business by 2027, was abruptly terminated. This marks the tenth Accenture contract or subcontract canceled under the Trump administration.

Notably, federal contracts accounted for about 8% of its global revenue and 16% of its revenue in the U.S. in FY24.

Wall Street Analysts Cut Price Targets on ACN Stock

Amid growing uncertainty, Wall Street analysts have lowered their price targets for ACN stock.

Four-star-rated analyst Bryan Bergin at TD Cowen reduced his price target to $365 from $394 while keeping his Buy rating. Bergin acknowledged that federal procurement pressures tied to DOGE are headwinds for the company.

Meanwhile, four-star-rated analyst James Schneider from Goldman Sachs highlighted Accenture’s solid revenue in a tough market. He believes that the post-Q2 stock selloff and FY25 guidance cut seem overdone, and the cautious stance reflects broader macro uncertainty. Schneider lowered his price target from $430 to $415 with a Buy rating.

Is ACN a Good Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on ACN stock based on 12 Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. At $393.94, the average Accenture share price target implies about 31% upside potential from current levels.

Year-to-date, ACN stock has declined over 14%.

data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Tesla Stock Caught in a Swath of Vandalism Protests and Record-High Trade-Ins
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock is caught in a swath of vandalism protests and record-high trade ins as the backlash against CEO Elon Musk intensifies daily. Telsa is one of the few brands closely attached to its CEO. People perceive Musk's actions as a reflection of Tesla's policies and vary their loyalty based on them.

Tesla owners and shareholders are disgruntled by the billionaire CEO's close ties with President Trump, cutting down of government staff, and his perceived lack of focus on Tesla's operations, which has impacted global sales. TSLA stock has already lost 41.5% year-to-date, owing to the growing backlash.   

"Tesla Takedown" Eyes Biggest Day of Action on March 29

A group called "Tesla Takedown" is eyeing its biggest day of action on March 29, with over 500 events planned worldwide, including demonstrations at 277 Tesla stores and Supercharger stations. The group reportedly includes organizers, filmmakers, members of Congress, and others. Unfortunately, the EV maker has been caught in the crossfire between Musk and the protesters, since a majority of Musk's wealth is tied to his stake in Tesla.

The protests have turned violent, with burning down of Tesla EVs, shootings at Tesla stores and Supercharger stations as well as damaging Tesla owners' vehicles. These acts have been described as "domestic terrorism" by officials, including the President, with the FBI now investigating the matter. Meanwhile, Musk has ramped up security at Tesla stores, deploying more cops outside high-profile store locations and using Sentry Mode on all EVs to help catch the perpetrators in act. The Sentry Mode on Tesla EVs uses its cameras and sensors to monitor the surroundings for potential threats.

Tesla Trade-Ins Occur at Record Levels

According to data from Edmunds, a national car shopping portal, Tesla owners are trading in their EVs at record levels, making March one of the worst months ever. Tesla autos from model year 2017 or newer makes accounted for 1.4% of total autos traded until March 15, up from 0.4% for the full month of March 2024. Analysts at Edmunds expect the figure to grow dramatically in the second half. In February, the figure stood at 1.2%.

Consumers are trading in their Tesla EVs for either other used cars or new vehicles from other brands. Additionally, the data showed that the demand for new Tesla EVs in February dropped to its lowest level (1.8%) since October 2022, the month when Musk the acquired social media platform Twitter, now X.

Commenting on the record trade-ins, Jessica Caldwell, head of insights at Edmunds, stated that Tesla's downfall could well be an opportunity for other legacy brands and even new EV startups to capture the loyalty of protesting Tesla owners and first-time EV buyers. She added that automakers offering competitive pricing, advanced technology, and even fewer controversies could do the magic for EV buyers.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 14 Buys, 11 Holds, and 11 Sell ratings. Also, the average Tesla price target of $336.04 implies 42.2% upside potential from current levels.

See more TSLA analyst ratings

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Says He Was "Wrong" About Timeline for Quantum Computing
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang is doing a mea culpa.

The head of the leading chipmaker says he was wrong in earlier comments he made about the future timeline of quantum computing. Huang caused a firestorm of controversy earlier this year when he cast doubt on whether useful quantum computers would be available within the next 15 years.

Huang’s remarks, which seemed to disparage the development of quantum computers led to a decline in the stocks of leading quantum computer companies such as Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and IonQ (IONQ). It also led many people in the quantum computing space to clap back at Huang and outline all the reasons he was wrong about the industry.

Quantum Day Event

At Nvidia's “Quantum Day” event, which is part of the company's annual GTC developer conference, Huang admitted that his comments concerning the development of quantum computers came out wrong. “This is the first event in history where a company CEO invites all of the guests to explain why he was wrong,” said Huang to a live audience that included representatives from 12 quantum companies.

Huang previously said that useful quantum computers were still about 20 years away, at the earliest. But now, Huang has walked back those remarks. The Nvidia CEO may have a big reason to now embrace quantum technology. As quantum computers are built, much of the research on them is done through simulators on powerful computers, like those that Nvidia sells and power with its microchips.

Nvidia said earlier this week that it plans to build a research center in Boston to allow quantum companies to collaborate with researchers at Harvard and the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT). NVDA stock has declined 12% so far this year.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

The stock of Nvidia has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average NVDA price target of $177.23 implies 51.87% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on NVDA stock

data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: NKE Earnings: Nike Manages to Beat Wall Street Targets Despite 9% Sales Plunge
data.description:
Sneaker and athletic apparel giant Nike (NKE) has managed to beat Wall Street’s targets for its fourth-quarter 2024 financial results despite the fact that its sales declined 9% from a year earlier.

The Oregon-based company known for its distinctive Swoosh logo reported Q4 2024 earnings per share (EPS) of $0.54, which was far ahead of the $0.30 consensus expectation of analysts. Revenue in the quarter came in at $11.27 billion, which was ahead of the $11.01 billion forecast on Wall Street.

The top and bottom line beats come despite the fact that Nike’s overall sales during the year-end holiday quarter fell 9% from the same period in 2023. Continued weakness in China, where Nike has a substantial presence, was blamed for the sales decline. Sales in China during the final months of last year plunged 17% amid an economic slowdown in the nation of 1.4 billion people.

‘Win Now’

Nike CEO Elliott Hill said in the company’s earnings statement that “the progress we made against the 'Win Now' strategic priorities we committed to 90 days ago reinforces my confidence that we are on the right path." He added that Nike remains committed to getting back to its roots focused on “sports” and “athlete storytelling.”

Nike pulled its guidance during its previous earnings report last October, and the company did not provide detailed guidance in its latest financial results, though it may do so during an earnings call with analysts and media. Hill, who became CEO last year, is engaged in a turnaround strategy at the company, and Nike has said that those turnaround efforts will pressure sales and profitability in the short term.

NKE stock has declined 27% over the last 12 months.

Is NKE Stock a Buy?

Nike's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 28 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and 12 Hold recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NKE price target of $88.71 implies 23.45% upside from current levels. These ratings are likely to change after the company’s latest financial results.

Read more analyst ratings on NKE stock

data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: MU Earnings: Micron Jumps on Solid Q2 Results
data.description:
Shares of chipmaker Micron (MU) jumped in after-hours trading after the company reported earnings for its second quarter of Fiscal Year 2025 that was accompanied by mixed guidance. Earnings per share came in at $1.56, which beat analysts’ consensus estimate of $1.43 per share. Sales increased by 38.3% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $8.05 billion. This also beat estimates of 7.9 billion.

In addition, the firm reported an adjusted operating income margin of 22%, while operating cash flow came in at $3.94 billion versus the $3.24 billion in the prior quarter and the $1.22 billion for the same period last year.

Q3 2025 Outlook

Micron Technology’s CEO, Sanjay Mehrotra, attributed the solid results to strong data center sales. Mehrotra also highlighted the advancements that Micron has made in memory technology and predicted that revenue in the upcoming quarter will hit a company record. Indeed, revenue is expected to be between $8.6 billion and $9 billion, with the midpoint significantly above the estimated $8.48 billion.

This revenue beat likely led to the jump in share price in after-hours, as it seems like it was enough to overshadow the company’s EPS outlook. For reference, Micron projects adjusted earnings in the range of $1.27 to $1.47 per share – a miss compared to the $1.52 per share consensus estimate.

Investor Sentiment for MU Stock

The sentiment among TipRanks investors is currently Very Negative. Out of the 792,477 portfolios tracked by TipRanks, 3.8% hold MU stock. In addition, the average portfolio weighting allocated towards MU among those who do have a position is 5.36%. This suggests that investors of the company are confident about its future. However, in the last 30 days, 6.1% of those holding the stock decreased their positions.

Is MU a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MU stock based on 14 Buys, one Hold, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average MU price target of $128.40 per share implies 24.7% upside potential. However, it’s worth noting that estimates will likely change following today’s earnings report.

See more MU analyst ratings

data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Short Sellers Earn $15B Betting Against TSLA and NVDA Stocks
data.description:
Short sellers have made big profits so far in 2025 as some of the market's biggest stocks have dropped. According to S3 Partners, betting against Tesla (TSLA) and Nvidia (NVDA) has earned short sellers $15 billion so far this year. Tesla short sellers alone made nearly $11 billion, as the stock has fallen 40% since January. Indeed, investors are worried that CEO Elon Musk is too focused on government work with the Trump administration, which could hurt Tesla's brand and sales.

Other major tech stocks are also struggling. In fact, the “Magnificent Seven” group, which includes Apple (AAPL), Alphabet (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta (META), is falling behind the S&P 500 (SPX) more than it has since 2022. In addition, short sellers have gained nearly $5 billion betting against Apple, which has seen its stock fall 14% this year. Investors are becoming more cautious as concerns about a slowing economy and the impact of Trump's new tariffs continue to grow.

Tech companies are also facing criticism for spending heavily on AI, with some doubting whether those investments will actually pay off. Some stocks, including Nvidia, saw major drops after China's DeepSeek released a cheaper AI model. Many of these stocks are now 20% below their recent highs, and investors are wondering if the excitement around them will return. Despite the sell-off, BMO Capital Markets strategist Brian Belski believes that these companies are still key players in the U.S. stock market and will remain important for long-term growth.

Which Magnificent Seven Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the magnificent stocks mentioned above, analysts think that NVDA stock has the most room to run. In fact, NVDA's average price target of $176.81 per share implies more than 48% upside potential. On the other hand, analysts expect the least from AAPL stock, as its average price target of $249.88 equates to a gain of 17%.

See more NVDA analyst ratings

data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Staking Could Be a 'Game Changer' for Ethereum (ETH) ETFs, Says BlackRock
data.description:
The addition of staking could boost investors’ appetite for Ethereum (ETH) exchange-traded funds (ETFs), says Robert Mitchnick, head of digital assets at BlackRock (BLK).

Speaking at the Digital Asset Summit in New York City, Mitchnick acknowledged that interest in Ethereum ETFs has been “meh” since they launched last summer. However, he said that the inability to earn a staking yield on the funds is likely holding investors back.

"There's obviously a next phase in the potential evolution of Ether ETFs," he said, adding that "an ETF, it's turned out, has been a really, really compelling vehicle through which to hold Bitcoin (BTC) for lots of different investor types. There's no question it's less perfect for ETH today without staking. A staking yield is a meaningful part of how you can generate investment return in this space.”

Passive Yield

Staking is a way for investors to earn passive yield on their cryptocurrency holdings by locking tokens up on the network for a set period of time. It allows investors to put their crypto to work if they're not planning to sell it anytime soon. So far, staking isn’t available for spot ETH and BTC ETFs.

Mitchnick said that, unfortunately, staking is “not a particularly easy problem” to fix. To date, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has viewed some staking services as potential unregistered securities offerings under the Howey Test – which is used to determine whether an asset is an investment contract and therefore a security.

However, Mitchnick is now more optimistic about the addition of staking under a crypto friendly SEC. Ethereum has been one of the most beaten up cryptos in recent months and is down nearly 40% this year. BlackRock is the issuer of the iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHA).

Is ETH a Buy?

Most Wall Street firms don't offer ratings or price targets on Ethereum, so we'll look at the cryptocurrency's three-month performance instead. As one can see in the chart below, the price of ETH has declined 39% in the last 12 weeks.

Questions or Comments about the article? Write to editor@tipranks.com
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Is the Best Bet in AI Search, Says Five-Star Analyst
data.description:
Despite rising competitive threats, Google parent company Alphabet (GOOGL) is still investors best bet when it comes to artificial intelligence (AI) enabled search engines, says a top Wall Street analyst.

Mark Kelley, a five-star rated analyst at investment bank Stifel Financial Corp. (SF), says that concerns Alphabet’s dominance in online search is under threat are overblown and that the company remains “best positioned in the AI Agent arena, with its Gemini Advanced product capable of handling queries in a conversational manner.”

Kelley also notes that Alphabet should continue to benefit from the “household nature of the Google brand.” Some analysts and investors have been wringing their hands lately over concerns that Google could lose its title as the king of search due to a number of new AI upstarts in the search engine space.

Buy Rating

Kelley rates GOOGL stock a Buy with a $225 price target, which is nearly 40% above where the shares are currently trading. The analyst highlighted several recent AI advancement from Alphabet that should help keep it ahead of the pack for the foreseeable future.

These include “Project Mariner,” which can help users complete tasks on their computer in real time, and “Jules,” a coding agent, which is meant to help coders handle bug fixes and other tasks in real time. Through its Google Cloud unit, Alphabet also offers the “Vertex AI Agent Builder” that is meant to be used to create AI agents and applications.

GOOGL stock has declined 14% so far this year.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Alphabet has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 37 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 27 Buy and 10 Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average GOOGL price target of $215.85 implies 28.41% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on GOOGL stock
